critical_weaknesses.title: Severe cohort/protocol confounding likely explains the high AUC
QC and exclusions are ad hoc and potentially label-biased

delta_since_round1: W1 quantified (§13 contrast-only ablation + §14.3 protocol AUC=1.000)
W2 resolved (patient-level audit: 282 cases = 282 unique patients, 0 leakage)
W6 partial (fold-level paired bootstrap + Wilcoxon; case-level DeLong pending)

W1.reason: Protocol-matched eval has only 26 nonPH negatives; label/protocol near-perfectly entangled in data

W2.reason: Audit confirms patient-disjoint; external validity still absent

W4.reason: mm-coords / ct_density / Strahler cycles still not fixed in v2 cache

W6.reason: Fold-level tests only; case-level DeLong missing

W7.reason: Scoped to appendix; QC still pending

W8.reason: No env lockfile, no kimimaro pin, no one-command rebuild

strengths: Protocol is trivially decodable from scalar lung features (AUC=1.000); disease AUC drops 0.898→0.678 under protocol balancing (§14.3)
Patient-disjoint splits backed by explicit audit (zero leakage)
history.reviewer_notes_for_memory: Round 2 confirms central Round 1 suspicion: the dataset is fundamentally acquisition-confounded (protocol nearly determines label per data/case_protocol.csv), and the main full-cohort AUCs are not clinically defensible. The contrast-only experiment is underpowered (26 negatives) and cannot rescue the claim. Next round must (i) make protocol-matched performance primary with adequate sample, (ii) run protocol-decodability on the *exact* GCN/cache features, (iii) deliver anatomical validation of graph construction (TEASAR/overlays), and (iv) upgrade inference to case-level paired AUC (DeLong/paired bootstrap) plus reproducibility artifacts.

notes: Round 2 verdict: 3/10 reject. W1 partially addressed (quantified but not mitigated); W2 fully addressed. Round 3 priorities per reviewer: (a) protocol decodability from GCN-internal features, (b) TEASAR overlay QC, (c) case-level DeLong, (d) W8 reproducibility.
